title: Integrate approved Phase3A polish slices into prep/v0.1.1-alpha
why: These changes (--json, branch context in doctor, latest.md for briefs/reviews, get_current_branch) are narrow, additive, and already dogfooded. They improve public usability without changing the CLI contract.
decision: Port 5 approved slices: mission show --json, doctor --json + branch context, brief latest.md, review latest.md + force=True, review weekly --json, get_current_branch helper

title: Public-surface cleanup for v0.1.1-alpha PR
why: README and public docs used internal phase/process language that would confuse external readers. Cleaning these up before creating the alpha PR reduces friction for new users.
decision: Strip Phase 1/2/3 references from README, remove dogfood/validation-lane language, rewrite as user-facing alpha product README; create draft PR from prep/v0.1.1-alpha to main; produce branch cleanup report.

title: Final public-surface cleanup for v0.1.1-alpha alpha PR
why: README and docs still carry internal build-journal energy. Need a professional public-alpha face: centered header, clean tagline, docs index in docs/README.md, and clear separation between user-facing and internal docs.
decision: Rewrite README with GitHub-friendly formatting (centered header, tagline, badge row, clean sections). Create docs/README.md as documentation index separating public-facing from internal/dev docs. Update SPINE_BRANCH_CLEANUP_REPORT.md with accurate remote branch data.

title: Add --cwd to Phase 2 commands
why: SPINE_ROOT env var is process-global and requires shell setup; --cwd enables explicit per-invocation targeting for external repos
decision: Add --cwd PATH option to all Phase 2 commands that lack it, following the pattern already established in spine review weekly and spine init
alternatives: add global --cwd at top-level app callback

title: Add minimal CI pipeline (Issue #6)
why: Public alpha needs automated quality checks on PRs and pushes; closes the 'always-passing' gap in branch protection
decision: GitHub Actions ci.yml: ruff lint + pytest on push/PR to main; ruff added as dev dependency with E/F/W rules

title: README onboarding polish for v0.1.2
why: External-repo usage was unclear: installation steps missing, --cwd only documented for init (stale after PR #11), SPINE_ROOT presented as primary external-repo pattern instead of --cwd, stale Known Limitations and What's Next sections.
decision: Update README: add Installation section, rewrite Quickstart to show --cwd-first external-repo flow, make --cwd the primary recommended pattern for all commands, remove stale limitations and what's-next items, fix Python version badge and test count.

title: Phase 3A planning normalization (Issue #10)
why: v0.1.2 stabilization is complete. SPINE is in public alpha. The canonical spec and planning docs contained stale Phase 2 bootstrap-era language. Correct internal truth before any implementation work begins on Phase 3A.
decision: Normalize all planning/spec docs for v0.2 / Phase 3A: correct stale phase language in SPINE_OFFICIAL_SPEC_v0.1.md, strengthen SPINE_PHASE3A_v0.2_SPEC.md with portability framing and discipline tax lens, align STATUS/ROADMAP/BACKLOG to current reality. No code changes. No feature implementation.
alternatives: Wait until a developer starts Phase 3A before normalizing — rejected
as stale docs would cause drift in implementation scope from day one.

title: Standardise --cwd > SPINE_ROOT > cwd targeting contract (Issue #15)
why: Ambiguous target resolution was a discipline tax: SPINE_ROOT was silently winning over --cwd, making explicit targeting unreliable. This created risk of writes to the wrong repo and eroded operator trust.
decision: Fix resolve_roots() to honour --cwd > SPINE_ROOT > cwd precedence. Add path-existence validation for SPINE_ROOT. Improve all error messages to state the resolved target, its source, and the corrective action. Standardise --cwd help text across all commands.

title: Issue #16: Repo/branch context visibility
why: Operators need to know exactly which repo, branch, and default branch SPINE is operating on for drift scan, review, brief, and mission commands to trust the output.
decision: Added get_default_branch() and format_context_line() to utils/paths.py. Applied consistent context header (repo/branch/default) to mission show, drift scan, brief, review weekly, doctor. Refactored drift_service to use shared utility.

title: Issue #18: Bootstrap polish + discipline-tax ergonomics
why: Reduces operator friction on first run and repeated use: stale template content, confusing next-steps panel, absolute paths in doctor errors, tautological help text all addressed
decision: Update init templates (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) to reflect Phase 3A commands; improve init next-steps panel with doctor+git-commit steps; fix doctor absolute-path bug; improve mission/spine-missing error messages; unhide --cwd in init; update top-level help text

title: Issue #23: Artifact ergonomics contract
why: Agents and CI need predictable artifact locations without filesystem guessing. Formalizes what was already partially in place.
decision: Add artifact_manifest.json updated on each brief/review generation; document deterministic naming (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS for briefs, YYYY-MM-DD for reviews); latest.md alias is a regular file. No dashboard, no remote sync, no major CLI expansion.

title: Issue #24: create external-repo onboarding docs
why: SPINE cannot exit alpha if external users must infer the workflow from internal repo history; a dedicated public doc is the minimum viable onboarding surface
decision: Create docs/external-repo-onboarding.md covering quickstart, governance walkthrough, --cwd vs SPINE_ROOT targeting, verification, and FAQ; update docs/README.md, docs/SPINE_STATUS.md, docs/SPINE_FEATURE_BACKLOG.md, and README.md accordingly

title: Alpha exit validated — Issue #25 gate matrix passes
why: All 21 alpha-exit gates evaluated. 20 pass, 1 non-blocking partial (no --json on evidence/decision add). External repo workflow, error signals, bootstrap, artifact ergonomics, repeated-use session, and anti-drift gates all confirmed. No blocking failures.
decision: Declare alpha exit validated. v0.2.0-beta is justified. Open Beta milestone.

title: Post-beta normalization pass
why: Repo docs still contained alpha-exit-era wording after v0.2.0-beta was released. README badge said alpha, ROADMAP described Phase 3A as the active phase, STATUS still listed 'declare beta' as the next priority, FEATURE_BACKLOG had a duplicate entry and stale Phase 3B intro, SPINE_ROADMAP.md was extremely stale. This pass brings all tracking and public-facing docs into sync with the actual release state.
decision: Normalize all docs and tracking files to reflect v0.2.0-beta reality: beta is active, alpha exit is complete, Phase 3A is done.

title: Implement spine check before-pr via composition of DoctorService
why: Issue #31 requires a bounded preflight checkpoint. Composing existing DoctorService and reading drift/evidence/decisions JSONL directly avoids code duplication and keeps the surface area minimal.
decision: Add CheckService that composes DoctorService + lightweight JSONL reads. Add check_cmd.py CLI layer. Exit 0=pass, exit 1=review_recommended, exit 2=context failure. No state mutation.

title: Implement spine review handoff as read-only governance summary
why: Issue #32: handoff boundary friction needs an explicit, bounded summary command. Placed under review group for natural fit alongside review weekly.
decision: Add spine review handoff subcommand. HandoffService reads mission/evidence/decisions/drift from .spine/. Stdout-only (no file write), consistent with check before-pr precedent. Supports --cwd, --json, --days.

title: Implement Issue #33: draftable governance records (Beta)
why: Draft records reduce governance discipline tax by allowing provisional entries without silently mutating canonical repo truth. Operators retain full authority; promotion is always explicit via spine drafts confirm.
decision: Add --draft flag to evidence add and decision add; store drafts under .spine/drafts/; provide spine drafts list and spine drafts confirm <id> for explicit promotion; exclude drafts from all canonical governance surfaces by default.
alternatives: Auto-promote drafts after timeout (rejected: silent mutation)
In-memory staging only (rejected: not persistent or auditable)

title: Issue #34: use pre-push as the SPINE hook type
why: pre-push fires once per push (immediately before PR opening), aligns with spine check before-pr intent, and is the cleanest native git hook for preflight governance enforcement without being too frequent (pre-commit) or unrelated (commit-msg)
decision: Install spine check before-pr into .git/hooks/pre-push via spine hooks install; support --ignore-failure for non-blocking mode; refuse to overwrite non-SPINE hooks

title: Conduct Beta Bug Hunt / Regression Audit on main
why: Audit main branch pre-feature queue to identify bugs, regressions, docs drift, contract inconsistencies, and workflow traps before proceeding with remaining Beta issues (#36, #37, #38)
decision: Run full audit session covering CLI behavior, file/artifact contracts, public docs, workflows, and agent-friendliness; document findings in docs/SPINE_BETA_BUG_AUDIT_REPORT.md

title: Beta blocker stabilization: fix #43 #44 #45
why: Shipped checkpoint and hook workflow broken: check before-pr exited 1 on healthy repos, hook script used bare 'spine' (not in PATH), AGENTS.md template shipped two invalid CLI commands. Beta queue blocked.
decision: Three narrow fixes: (1) doctor warnings demoted to advisory-only in _check_doctor_health — only errors block; (2) hook script changed from 'spine check before-pr' to 'uv run spine check before-pr'; (3) AGENTS.md template corrected: 'opportunity score' and 'brief --target claude'. Regression tests added for all three.

title: Issue #36: implement mission refine draft flow
why: Mission framing is highest-discipline part of SPINE. Operators need to explore refinements without accidentally mutating canonical truth.
decision: Add spine mission refine (draft creation at .spine/drafts/missions/) and spine mission confirm (explicit promotion). No silent mutation of mission.yaml. Draft-first, operator-confirms pattern consistent with existing draft mechanics from #33.

title: Add compatibility and integration guide (Issue #37)
why: SPINE's complementary role relative to Claude Code, oh-my-claudecode, and Superpowers was not explicitly documented. Without this, users building with those tools could mistake SPINE for a competing orchestration surface rather than a governance layer that works with them.
decision: Create docs/SPINE_INTEGRATIONS.md as the public-facing compatibility guide. State the explicit compatibility rule. Add workflow examples for Claude Code, oh-my-claudecode, and Superpowers. Link from README.md, docs/README.md, and external-repo-onboarding.md.
alternatives: Inline the guidance into README.md; defer to Phase 3B

title: Add deterministic fixture/harness layer for Beta Issue #38
why: Beta regression risk grows as features accumulate. Explicit fixtures make core contracts machine-verifiable and human-readable without expanding SPINE into a framework.
decision: Introduce tests/fixtures/ with YAML/JSON fixtures and tests/test_fixture_contracts.py harness covering mission.yaml, artifact_manifest.json, JSON output shapes, exit codes, and --cwd targeting.

title: Add --json to all write-oriented CLI commands (Issue #49)
why: Agents need stable, machine-readable output contracts when writing governance records. Without --json, agents must scrape human-readable output or skip write flows entirely.
decision: Added --json flag to evidence add, decision add, drafts list, drafts confirm, mission refine, mission confirm, and mission drafts. Output shape: {ok: true, ...record} on success, {error, exit_code} on failure. Exit codes unchanged.
